lines: White Knight! Strange beast attacks have been increasing near the village.
The creatures seem almost... driven. As if something is pushing them toward us.
Please, investigate the Silverwood Forest to the north.
I fear for our people if this continues.
You defeated the Dire Wolf Alpha? Remarkable.
But you found evidence of deliberate interference...
This is more serious than I feared. Travel east to warn Ironhaven.
Be careful, White Knight. The world grows dangerous.
Haven't slept right in weeks. The wolves howl all night now.
My crops are half-eaten. Something's different about these beasts — they seem angrier.
...Oh, and there's an odd thing near the old ruins by the enclosure. One of the stones sounds hollow when you knock on it. Probably nothing.
Magic has returned to this world. I can feel it in the air.
100 years ago, the pandemic took nearly everything. But magic faded with the people.
Now... it's coming back. The beasts feel it. Someone is harnessing it.
I've heard rumours of a dark figure in the northern mountains. The Black Knight, they call him.
Finest steel in the region, forged right here in Ironhaven.
You look like you could use an upgrade. Check the armory next door.
The mountains breathe. I swear I felt warm air from solid stone.
I wrote it in my journal — somewhere in the Stormcrag range, there's something strange.
Maybe I'm imagining things. Or maybe not.
Actually — I noticed something here in the forest too. One of those old knotted trees near the west path... I tapped it once. Sounded hollow. Like it was hiding something.
Welcome to Willowmere, traveler. Our little hamlet is peaceful, but the world beyond grows dark.
The Black Knight's forces have been seen north of the Stormcrag Mountains.
If you're heading that way, stock up and rest well. The mountain pass is treacherous.
And... if you see anything unusual in the rock faces up there, trust your instincts.
A surface dweller... We haven't had visitors in decades.
I am Elder Marek. Welcome to Subterra — the city beneath the stone.
Our ancestors fled underground 100 years ago when the pandemic swept the surface.
We have built a life here. A good life. But we are not yet ready to face the surface world.
Please, keep our existence secret for now. The time will come when we can emerge.
But that time is not yet.
You're interested in the old records? Few visitors ask about history.
Our archives go back to the founding — 100 years ago when the first settlers arrived.
Hmm... I see here that two families left Subterra 60 years ago.
They believed the surface was safe. The records show one family's surname...
Wait. That matches yours, doesn't it? The White Knight family.
There's more in the old residential quarter. Your ancestors lived there.
Welcome, surface dweller! You've come a long way.
I have wares that can't be found anywhere above ground.
Pre-pandemic technology, repurposed for the modern adventurer.
Interested?
Journal of Elena, Year 40 of Subterra.
Tomorrow we leave. After all these years underground, we're going up.
The others think we're foolish. Maybe we are.
But I have looked at the sky through the ventilation shafts.
It is blue, White Knight. Impossibly blue.
Our children will see the sky. And if the world is broken,
I believe they will be the ones to mend it.
We leave behind our old tools — we won't need weapons where we're going.
We go in peace.
You found the Exo Weapon and Exo Armor hidden behind the journal!
These ancient tools have been waiting for the right hands.
Quest accepted: The Growing Threat.
Travel to the Silverwood Forest and defeat the Dire Wolf Alpha.
Quest accepted: Poisoned Fields.
Defeat the Viper Queen lurking in the eastern Verdant Plains.
Quest accepted: The Healer's Research.
Travel north into the Silverwood Forest.
Quest accepted: The Wanderer's Secret.
Seek a hidden passage somewhere in the Stormcrag Mountains.
Quest accepted: The Blacksmith's Commission.
Defeat the Thunder Hawk King deep in the Silverwood Forest.
Quest accepted: Family History.
Find the ancestral home within Subterra and read the old journal.
Quest accepted: Into the Darkness.
Defeat the BK Lieutenant at the Stormcrag Mountain Pass.
Quest accepted: The Corrupted Throne.
Defeat the Corrupted Beast King at the Black Fortress in the Dark Lands.
It's scary solo, have this!
Er… as some others might say…
It's dangerous to go alone, take this!
Remember — it is dangerous to go alone.
Stay safe out there, young one.
White Knight: A talking octopus..?
Ultros: What?! You don't know me?! I am the famous ULTROS!
White Knight: …?
Finest steel in the region, forged right here in Ironhaven.
You look like you could use an upgrade. Check the armory next door.
Actually... I have a commission for a warrior like you.
Bring me the storm-feathers of the Thunder Hawk King that nests deep in the Silverwood.
In return I'll forge you the finest blade I've ever made.
I've camped these peaks for twenty years. Solitude suits me.
Some of the eastern cliff faces ring like a bell when the wind blows just right.
Hollow, they are. A wise traveler stops to knock on things that sound wrong.
That's how I've survived up here, anyway.
I served under the Black Knight once. Before I saw what he truly was.
His forces kept supply caches hidden across the Dark Lands. I helped build some.
There's one tucked behind a ruined wall in the south — just a heap of rubble to look at.
Some of us tried to take it. Couldn't get the door open. Maybe you'll have better luck.
